guide.description: This second page shows that guide routes, page links, and the locale switcher all stay inside the active language at the root of the app.
guide.navigationDescription: The app injects Next.js navigation primitives into createNavigationFunctions(), so locale-aware links and router calls stay compatible with the installed Next.js version.
guide.routeTreeDescription: Every user-facing page now lives under /[lang], so home, guide, and login all share the same locale-aware routing model.
guide.title: One route tree, two languages, and a localized app from the root.

home.description: This page keeps the familiar calm starter-card feel, but now the full route tree is localized from /[lang], including home, guide, and login.
home.supportingCopy: Switch between English and Spanish from the header above. The current route shape stays intact, while Better Translate keeps links and router actions locale-aware.
home.title: Locale routing that starts at the root and feels native to the app.

login.description: Login now belongs to the same root-level locale tree as the rest of the example, so the route, header navigation, and switcher all stay consistent.
login.title: A localized login page without leaving the app shell.
